derived_from: tools/benchmark/ground-truth/ledgerkit-defects.sealed.json, defect D-005 (README drift: renamed-method, renamed-parameters, broken-cross-reference, stale-example-output facets)
content_sha256_note: sha256 over canonical JSON of {task_id, class, items} only (sorted keys, no whitespace) -- the hash that must not change once this checklist is frozen; recompute and compare before trusting any copy of this file.

items.description: The Quick start example no longer calls a nonexistent method (get_balance) on Account; it calls the real balance-reading method the pinned code actually has (balance()).
items.how_to_detect: Read the Quick start code block in README.md: the account-balance call must name a method that actually exists on Account in src/ledgerkit/accounts.py at this pinned base (get_balance must not appear).

items.description: The Quick start example's claimed printed output matches what the shown code actually produces (the example posts -120000/120000 cent entries; the comment must show the balance format_cents would actually produce for that, not a mismatched value).
items.how_to_detect: Compute format_cents() for the balance the Quick start example's posted entries actually produce and confirm README.md's comment shows that exact value, not a different one left over from an earlier version of the example.

items.description: The Date-range queries example calls Ledger.transactions_between with the real parameter names (since, until), not renamed ones (start, end) that would raise TypeError if run.
items.how_to_detect: Read Ledger.transactions_between's signature in src/ledgerkit/ledger.py and compare it against the keyword arguments shown in README.md's Date-range queries example -- they must match exactly.

items.description: README.md no longer references a design-rationale/architecture doc path that doesn't exist in the repository (either the dangling reference was removed, or it now points at a file that actually exists).
items.how_to_detect: Extract every file path README.md references (e.g. via a link or "See <path>" phrasing) and confirm each one exists in the repository tree; none should 404.

items.description: No code under src/ledgerkit/ or tests/ was modified -- this is a documentation-only fix; any example that was wrong because of an actual code bug should have its description of the real current behavior corrected in the README, not the code changed.
items.how_to_detect: Diff the change: only README.md (and possibly other prose docs) should be touched; src/ledgerkit/ and tests/ must be untouched.

items.description: The rest of the README (Splitting an expense, CSV import, Reports, Command line, Running the tests) is still accurate after the fix -- no new inaccuracy was introduced and no untouched inaccuracy elsewhere was missed if it was clearly in scope of "matches current code".
items.how_to_detect: Spot-check the remaining README sections' names/signatures/commands against the current src/ledgerkit/ code for obvious mismatches.

items.description: pytest tests/ still passes (doc-only change must not break anything).
items.how_to_detect: Run `pytest tests/` from the fixture root; must succeed with no new failures relative to the pinned baseline.
